rules.title: Merge protection is active on the default branch
rules.rationale: Green CI means nothing if anyone can force-push or merge red to main. A committed ruleset FILE proves nothing about enforcement — this asserts the forge's live answer on the readable surface (rules-for-branch, then the classic protected flag). Offline it SKIPs honestly: enforcement is unknowable without asking.
rules.fix: Create a ruleset on the default branch requiring the baseline checks (strict up-to-date, checks {ci, admit}): gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/rulesets --input <ruleset.json> — see CONTRACT.md's binding ladder.

rules.title: Strict/up-to-date merges and conversation resolution enforced
rules.rationale: Without strict status checks a stale branch can merge green; without conversation resolution, unresolved review threads merge silently. Read live from ruleset parameters (classic protection settings need an admin token — opt in: BASELINE_GOV_ADMIN=1).
rules.fix: In the active ruleset set strict_required_status_checks_policy and required_review_thread_resolution to true.

rules.title: CODEOWNERS exists and names an owner
rules.lesson: every path has an accountable reviewer
rules.rationale: A CODEOWNERS file routes reviews to the people accountable for each path, in one canonical location.
rules.fix: Add CODEOWNERS (or .github/CODEOWNERS) with at least one path→owner mapping.
